title: Bat Removal & Exclusion in Hudson Heights, Manhattan | $900 – $2,500
metaDescription: Professional bat removal and exclusion services in Hudson Heights, Manhattan. Safe, humane bat control for homes and buildings. Licensed experts. Call today for a free estimate!
intro: Hudson Heights residents facing bat infestations need professional removal services that prioritize safety and effective exclusion. Our bat removal specialists serve Hudson Heights with humane, state-compliant solutions that protect your home and family from bat-related health risks. From pre-war buildings to modern residences near Fort Tryon Park, we provide comprehensive bat exclusion services tailored to your property's unique needs.
whyUs: Best Pest Control NYC brings over a decade of experience serving Hudson Heights with specialized bat removal and exclusion techniques. Our licensed technicians use humane methods, provide thorough attic inspections, seal all entry points, and offer warranties on our exclusion work to ensure bats don't return to your Manhattan home.
localContext: Hudson Heights' elevated terrain and proximity to natural areas like Fort Tryon Park and Inwood Hill Park make it particularly susceptible to bat activity, especially in older apartment buildings and historic homes along Cabrini Boulevard. The neighborhood's mix of pre-war architecture and hillside properties often feature the roof gaps, attic spaces, and ventilation openings that bats seek for roosting sites.

faqs.question: How much does bat removal cost in Hudson Heights?
faqs.answer: Bat removal and exclusion services in Hudson Heights typically range from $900 to $2,500, depending on the size of the colony, number of entry points, and extent of exclusion work needed. We provide free inspections and detailed quotes before beginning any work.

faqs.question: Is bat removal legal in New York City?
faqs.answer: Yes, but it must be done humanely and in compliance with NYC and New York State regulations. Bats are protected species, so removal must occur outside of maternity season (June-August) when flightless young may be present. Our licensed technicians follow all legal requirements for safe, humane bat exclusion.

faqs.question: What are signs of bats in my Hudson Heights home?
faqs.answer: Common signs include squeaking or scratching sounds at dusk or dawn, droppings (guano) in attics or near entry points, grease marks around openings, strong ammonia-like odors, and seeing bats enter or exit your home at twilight. If you notice any of these signs, contact us immediately for an inspection.

faqs.question: How long does the bat exclusion process take?
faqs.answer: The complete bat exclusion process typically takes 2-4 weeks, depending on colony size and weather conditions. We install one-way exclusion devices that allow bats to leave but not return, then seal all entry points once we confirm all bats have exited. We also provide cleanup and sanitation services to remove guano and restore affected areas.
